rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Corstjens and Lal (2000) in its related-literature argument to establish that store brands can differentiate retailers, build loyalty, and improve retailer profits. It then contrasts and advances this line with its own vertical-differentiation model of multitier store brands, focusing on quality ordering, channel profit effects, and coordination. This is more than a passing mention because the cited work helps frame the conceptual starting point that the new model develops further.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Corstjens and Lal's claim that store brands can create retailer differentiation and profit benefits, extending the inquiry to multitier store-brand portfolios and endogenous quality positioning in a channel model. It broadens the prior line to additional outcomes such as entry deterrence, interlaced quality tiers, and channel coordination implications.
